dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won the day because Claude Code drew repeated firsthand endorsements for real coding and infrastructure work, while Anthropic's no-ads and trust framing added another positive thread even with some skepticism about how durable that stance will be [E1][E2][E4].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic led on both coverage and quality of discussion: multiple front-page threads independently described Claude Code as the tool people already rely on for shipping work, debugging infra, and even preservation-style reverse-engineering tasks, and the dedicated Anthropic post generated unusually favorable discussion around trust and avoiding ads [E1][E2][E4]. A reverse-engineering thread did include a direct complaint that Claude fabricated code and lost to Codex in one workflow [E5], but it was outweighed by the broader, repeated positive usage reports across the front page.

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex got direct praise for beating Claude in one reverse-engineering workflow and was also named alongside Claude and Gemini as clearly ahead of local coding models, but the most explicit ChatGPT discussion was split between search usefulness and worries about ads and trust [E6][E8][E7].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code kept surfacing as the assistant people already use for production coding, infra debugging, and niche restoration work, and Anthropic's trust/no-ads positioning landed well in its own thread even if some commenters doubted how long that posture will survive competitive pressure [E1][E2][E4].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini only showed up in one meaningful comparison, where commenters grouped it with Claude and Codex as still far stronger than current local coding models [E9].

evidence.summary: In the 'Claude Code for Infrastructure' Show HN, the submitter and multiple commenters said Claude Code already helps with real ops work, with one saying it had saved dozens of hours and made the job much less frustrating.

On 'Claude is a space to think', commenters treated Anthropic's no-ads/trust positioning as a meaningful differentiator and several described Claude Code as a workhorse for development and automation, though some questioned whether the values messaging would last.
The Claude Code quota-workaround thread foregrounded usage limits in the title, but the main comparison comment still argued that Claude remained far above local models on coding ability.

In the Crimsonland preservation thread, a commenter credited Claude Code with helping decode obsolete formats, understand original C++ behavior, and make a niche restoration project feasible with persistence and tokens.
In the Ghidra MCP thread, one commenter said Claude/Claude Code fabricated a lot of code during reverse engineering and that GPT 5.2 Codex produced better results.
The strongest direct OpenAI praise came in the Ghidra MCP thread, where a commenter said Cursor plus GPT 5.2 Codex gave the best reverse-engineering results after Claude had fabricated code.
In Anthropic's trust-themed post, ChatGPT was praised for search/deep web lookup and broad free access, but the same discussion repeatedly raised ads and trust concerns around OpenAI's direction.
In the Claude Code local-model thread, Codex was named alongside Claude and Gemini as still much more capable for coding than current local models.
Gemini's main signal came from the Claude Code quota thread, where commenters grouped Gemini with Claude and Codex as clearly stronger than local coding models.
